item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, ihp, this is pretty impressive. A worthy sequel to Andrew Swann's Proposal.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Went here before reading, panicked that djoric was dead and this is some tribute thing.

Congrats if that was intended.

item.title: Re: security issue
item.content:encoded: [REDACTED]

item.title: COOL!
item.content:encoded:
I've been shocked to be incoherent……I'm afraid I'll die one day（nope

WHAT AN INCREDIBLE META!!!!(again)

item.title: Maybe a typo or something
item.content:encoded:
While reading the Incident Report of SCP-5500-Omega-036 and 037, I found they are called "SCP-036-Omega and SCP-037-Omega". Is it a typo or by purpose?

Btw, it's a nice meta, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The hidden text in offset 4(Those transparent letters in "LIST"), are we supposed to see it when we highlight it? Because in Black Highlighter theme, you cannot see transparent letters just by highlighting it.

Maybe you can use text same color as the background? (I tried it in my sandbox and it works, and the color code is #F4F4F4.)

And also…I've just translated this into Chinese.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Big plus 1! It makes me wanna make a whole cannon out of this!

Death of the author's canon?

Nah. Still +1

item.title: Continuity Error
item.content:encoded:
Operation WALLBREAK, which Project S.W.A.N.N. is inspired by, occurs in 2023.

Time is 15:32 on April 1st, 2020.

Huh? Did they travel back in time to test the S.W.A.N.N. engine?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The amount of scale involved is comparable to the Ouroboros proposal. Unbelievable. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is interesting. I enjoyed the metafiction and Twine. The CYOA story was fun and fairly easy to navigate too.

I did notice a decent amount of spelling errors and missing spaces. There was also a lot of lip-chewing going on. I got Ending A, and it wasn't very satisfying either. Felt like a cop-out, if I'll be honest, but I appreciate the amount of work and planning that has to go into a project like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed it. Thanks for the incredible amount of time i guess you spent making this.

item.title: Re: Different Endings
item.content:encoded: Yes, it's different bits of evidence. If you want to get all the endings, you have to select different pieces of evidence.

item.title: Jesus Fourth-Wall-Breaking Harold Christ, this needs more +1s
item.content:encoded: I've seen some crazy articles in my 3 years reading the Wiki, but I’ve never seen shit as crazy as a complete fourth wall break and choose-your-own-story interactive like this. Nice job

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that B and B+ share the status as true ending.

item.title: holy crap
item.content:encoded: This article is amazing. How'd you make it interactive?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ihp, the author of this article, is an established author of many highly rated works. You can find their author page here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I remember this from the contest! I enjoy this article a ton, and I look forward to seeing what you can do in the future!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is there some sort of premium account I could get on this wiki that would allow me to upvote this more than once? Because I would pay for that. This really was just amazing. An incredible amount of work must have went into this. I'm the most easily distracted, ADHD person I know, and there was a solid two hours between me starting to read this story and getting to the end of it where I didn't look up from my phone once. My house could have caught on fire and I wouldn't have noticed.

Great job!

item.title: security issue
item.content:encoded:
Putting up here for visibility, there is an XSS vulnerability in the twine games restore from checkpoint function, A malevolent actor could craft a link to the twine game in a way that could hijack your browser in the same way another malicious link could. Web browsers are very well sandboxed these days so it is unlikely that real harm could be done, but still a good idea no not trust any links to the ywine game.

I have talked to Ihp and am working on a solution with him as we speak. Hopefully we can get something out in the next few days.

Yo, this is pretty fun to read. It hooked me on since the beginning. Amazing job :D

It's pretty well done and it feels like it was done with a lot of effort and love. Meta is cool and so is this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed a lot the entire article. 4th wall and meta aspects are one of my favourites themes for a story. And the cherry on the top it's the interactive final part (wich take me by surprise) wich I think that fits pretty well with the overall theme of the article and give the reader an essential role here. So I give a +1.

item.title: Different Endings
item.content:encoded: I got the C and M endings, but i’m unclear as to what you could do different to get A, B, B+. The only different things i noticed would be getting different evidence, but i didn’t think it’s that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe not, but we ain't all horror writers. Excuse me while I try to stop being mad at a fictional character, I mean sure, I ain't finished my first SCP yet but, i don't write horror. Ever

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got a walkthorugh? I got B+ first time, now got C…and I ain't gotten a "true ending"…I am bad at mysteries…at least when it involves PEOPLE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: LUnar True Vacuum is misspelled, and ending C broke…damnit, i got B first time..C can't be the true or best ending…dam,nit…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is insanely well done, and the amount of effort put into it deserves a +1 on that merit alone. But still, the utterly non-serious way the supposed 'threat' is conveyed makes me not want to give a vote on it as it stands.

Whilst this will likely win the contest either way, I feel like it would be a disservice to anyone else to actually vote for it due to how comically it treats what would otherwise be an existentially horrifying scenario to trump even things like 3812 or Swann itself. So, for now: no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lmao, I loved this. I even got to the "it's a mystery" link from the comments thing. I did, however, notice one typo. Did you mean Talloran, not Tallorian? It's somewhere in the choose-your-own-adventure section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really interested. And now, all I need is just the permission to watch. If you had an e-mail about someone who wants to watch, it's me. ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a forum thread on the wiki where the individual chapters have been compiled, but it's out of date and probably not that convenient to navigate. If you really, really want to read it, here's a link to the Google Doc where all… 100-something pages have been laid out for ease of reading. Note: the story is pure SCP wiki crackfic at its finest, and probably won't make a lick of sense to anyone who wasn't in the (very small) group of people who were involved in writing it. It does get to some pretty fun metaphysical fuckery near the middle and end, though, so if you're really interested in getting to that, stick with it :P. Hope you enjoy? (I feel weird self-advertising on someone else's article ack)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this article actually reminded me a lot of my (and several other site members') very own Skeleton Wars

If it's things that you made in the Wiki… do you had links ? I want to see it. ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+Opinions (spoilers)
– hide block

As weird as it might sound, this article actually reminded me a lot of my (and several other site members') very own Skeleton Wars. The focus it had on the relationship between reality and fictional universes, as well as the (admittedly very suspension of disbelief-heavy) premise it employed of the readers/authors being incorporated into the narrative thanks to the actions of the characters within it (who, of course, are fictional, and are being written by the very same author[s] who are supposedly being strung along by said characters) gave me a nice and much-needed throwback to a passion project of my own from way back in the day. That specific kind of pataphysical fuckery is always interesting to me, so coming to the realization that this article would be focusing on it really tickled my fancy.

Extremely niche and esoteric comparisons aside, I find myself a bit conflicted when it comes to this article. The mystery aspect, like in psul's A Death in Containment entry, is executed fantastically; a murder mystery involving the authors of the SCP wiki themselves is an amazingly cool idea, and in both endings the culprit's identity is clever and works well with the information you've collected up to that point. I also liked that the reader isn't guaranteed to get one of the two "actual" endings by just clicking random options and collecting unrelated clues — I put a lot of thought my first time through into choosing options that I felt made sense and would lead me to an actual answer, and finding out that my investigative efforts had actually been rewarded (rather than the article doing what lots of other interactive articles do and just giving the reader the payoff regardless of what choices they make) was a pleasant surprise. The buildup and transition to the Twine section of the article was surprisingly smooth and well-done too; normally I'd find myself objecting to an article shifting so heavily from pataphysical existential dread and horror (with bits of comedy sprinkled in) to full-on buddy-cop metafictional sleuthing, but in this case the shift was pulled off in such a way that it felt natural and unintrusive. And while it really doesn't need to be said (considering this is an article written by ihp), the dialogue, characterization, and interactions between Sinclair, Reynolds, and the reader-entity was consistently well-written, entertaining, and enjoyable, and kept the interactive portion of the mystery fun and engaging throughout.

That being said, I did have some issues with the article, mainly the payoff in both the "real" endings. Okay, I just replayed through the Twine section, and a lot of the complaints that I'd initially had (such as the endings not feeling super satisfying or conclusive despite being conceptually very clever and the prose itself in said endings feeling a bit anticlimactic) have either been addressed in recent edits or were solved by things I straight up missed. Namely, I'd somehow missed the final note on the "this page doesn't exist yet!" offset with the "click here" link, which wrapped up that ending much more nicely than my initial readthrough had suggested, as well as ending B+, which did more than enough to make the ending with Bright feel as impactful and satisfying as I felt it should have. Having gone through the article again, my no-vote has been changed to an enthusiastic up-vote, and I'll definitely be rooting for this entry to pull through and win the contest.

in other words, my b lmao

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know a lot of people think meta stuff is overdone at this point and to an extent that's valid. But I think this entry stands out as metafiction done right. I particularly liked the interactive portion of it and the various endings you can get.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
B+? Get hecced

I am slightly torn by this, but I also kind of love it? So when I say this might be the one meta skip that actually works for me, don't take it as glowing praise. I for one did like the Twine game, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AAAAAAAAAAAA! I love this, I love this, I love this SO MUCH! its long, and it took me forever to read because I tried to go in every possible direction even when there was an opportunity to move forward in the story from the point where I currently was in it. hmm… I missed B+ but managed to get every other ending. at some point I'll have to invest the time to try and get there (not right now, though… the are sooooo many contest entries and I have so little free time, but want to try to read as many as i can before the final results are tallied and a winner is then selected).

this was long but I had such a blast reading it that the time absolutely flew by, and I was sad when it ended. just when you start to wonder how anybody could possibly do anything new, you see something like this. sure, choose your own adventure isnt new, but I haven't seen it here yet, so it's new for this purpose.

freaking fantastic. this is the most fun I've had reading a 5k entry yet (and i think I've read +/- 30 at this point), a distinction which until now had been earned by an article that, while i really love it, I don't think is 5k material. this is great and I had so much fun, so thank you for writing in and putting in what was clearly a colossal amount of work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very ambitious and very fun to read, i thoroughly enjoyed it. My one problem is that this doesn't feel like it should be 5000. As good as it is, the X000s need, at least in my opinion, some sense of grandeur and the potential for major headcanon shifting and a more serious tone. For as good as this one was, I can't take it seriously enough to want to put it among the ranks of the other X000s. No vote for now, but will gladly upvote once this contest is over, good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the most extensive SCP articles I’ve ever read. The Foundationverse is up to like 5 levels of meta at this point and I am not complaining, I’m a sucker for metafiction. One of the strongest +1s I’ve ever given, good fucking job.

item.title: meh
item.content:encoded:
While I like the scope that this article takes, I abhor the concept. The SCP Wiiki is a horror website, and the reason it can actually intrigue people is the question of a "Could a Foundation such as this ever be real?" By making everything in the wiki the creation of horror writers, it takes out all of the existential horrors of the entities in question.

For me, this is a -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think Ihp forgot their own 001…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I take back what I said about Made in Heaven:

I think this might be the best article on the site in general?

+ show block
– hide block

Okay the Author List section has some SPAG issues like weird capitalization and missing spaces but thats all I've noticed so far

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

"Reynolds looks over the list. "Five-thousand of them, Christ. Most of the 4000s are gone over here. Don't you ever run out of ideas?"

Quite frequently. Some people are seeing metafiction and pataphysics as a crutch.

"I have to agree with Murphy Law on this." Sinclair sighed. "I never metafictional character I liked. No offense meant."

None taken. We're sick of it, too. As the commments on this are clear to point out."

Can't say I don't agree with this part. I want to hate it but it's good enough to get a +1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You forgot there are TWO Pataphysics 001 proposals. the I.H.Pickman Proposal based from Site 87 is likely more accurate for some of the authors: http://www.scp-wiki.net/i-h-p-proposal and is certainly true in my case. WHen I get my scp done. eventually. We might be in series 7 by then for all I know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this started out REALLY good. i LOVE meta content, i love swann's proposal, and this was doing a great job with the consequences of author death and how those would be felt in universe.

and then i got to the twine section.

holy MOLY, is this boring. and it just keeps GOING. and GOING. it wasn't enough to hold my attention. i get that it's fun for some, but i already hate point-and-click adventure type games, and now i have to attack this huge detective one just to read an article. and now, coming to the comments, i see there are multiple endings? geez.

this probably would have been better as a standalone release as opposed to a mainlist article, let alone a 5000 contest entry. it leaves me with the same bad taste in my mouth that 4205 did. but hey, what do i know, that one's the top rated entry from 2019, and this one by my count is currently leading the contest in upvotes.

-1.

Gotta say it, you got me good. With Swann's Proposal, killing off authors, travelling through various works in the wiki (and the Wanderer's Library), shutting off the fourth wall, and finally, when I thought it would be over, I got Ending C. Too goddamn genius, my fellow human.

Honestly, the only thing that bothered me was the amount of backtracking I had to do to get to my first ending, but hey, I'm not a gamer, so my point isn't really valid. The connection through previous fictional works didn't really frustrate me because I spent too much time reading this wiki.

Easily a +1. Go ahead, take it. You deserved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Finally there are more Apollyon Class SCPs. And it seems I'm one of the few people who like meta articles. I also personally like this SCP because it's overpowered (yes I like overpowered SCPs) and I also like the SCP to sometimes be a phenomenon rather than a creature.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Metafictional magic" LMAO
I feel like this is sometimes overdramatic and the gets silly, needs some tones adjustments.
Still +1, got B+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ending C still broke :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Going through this over and over again trying to get ending B is hell. I don't even care anymore. +1 for everything except the immensely janky Twine section. Also, what's the deal with the (presumably multiple) softlocks, the sandbox page, and the Checkpoint reached text? Overall, just a slog to go through over and over again. I'd really rather not have this exact iteration of this SCP be SCP-5000, but it is what it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm getting so tired of going all the way back to the start.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a spiritual successor to the 001, I think this works really well and expands on the concept in a very creative and original way. Great work, the choose your own adventure was super cool and must've been a lot of work, I got a B+ (poor bright :( ). I did feel that the characters guided the player a bit too strongly and that I felt more led along than like I was really participating, but that wasn't enough of a deterrent to hinder my enjoyment.

Big +1, that was super cool and I'd be very happy with this winning the 5k slot!

item.title: Fairly Major Revisions!
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so, I've done some things of questionable advisability.

  • Completely reworked Ending A.
  • Heavily revised and expanded upon Ending B, including adding Ending B+.
  • Revised the Wanderer's Library section somewhat.
  • Revised several clues leading to Ending B to make the culprit hopefully more obvious.
  • Fixed hopefully most of the major spelling errors.
  • Made steps to add a more neutral overall tone to the player character, so they're less me but with your name and more of a blank face.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
I legitimately thought I fixed most of the spelling errors in the latest version— turns out what I did instead was upload the raw Twine file instead of the rendered HTML. I went through every part of the document and corrected the spelling of everything that wasn't a command.

Beyond that, I managed to figure out why the errant > is appearing— Twine uses statements like «this», and I somehow added an extra > at the end of one of the statements every time it showed up.

I can't help with the rest, I'm afraid. Maybe after the contest. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the concept and the expanding upon of The Database, but there's a large amount of spelling/grammar errors. Novote for now.

item.title: If Micahel Bay did pataphysics...
item.content:encoded:
Concepts in here are appetizingly clever, like a controlled death as a way to interact with the higher pataphysical plane and the siphoning of consciousnesses. But I think this is ultimately not good.

The edgy, bombastic attempt of something like "Fuck this cold clinical tone shit," microcosms almost everything that is wrong about this article to me. The tonal two-stepping into and out of the 4th wall make for a disjointed read. I'm not sure that the memetic text needs to be there at all. Technobabble and a heavy-handed recruitment of pillars of site fandom serve to maim your originality and come off as artificial. Shouting-out the r/SCP is kind of a new low to me; maybe it's just the framing of this contest. The offsets here are tiring and are an impediment to the flow. They are also arguably not necessary. The internal monologues of the Twine are melodramatic and a big ask given the set up. For example, I'm more interested in what portions of the narrative might die given a particular meta-death, more than who did any killing. You don't endearingly lean on the 4th wall or skirt along the line between SCP and tale as well as other entries in this contest do, or say in your 001. In a medium and subject where treating that 4th wall with subtlety is probably pivotal, this reminds me of a Kool-aid man entrance.

It is a demonstrable fact that the site is trending towards pataphysics, and those who just don't like it had better find a way to deal. I personally am looking forward to it and I think you are likely one of those authors to lead the way. But this is absolute overkill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers:

+ show block
– hide block

It is meant to link to offset 4. If you look closely at the nonexistent page…

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
[+ Opinion]
– hide block

I didn't really enjoy this. I've not got an issue with meta or pataphysics in articles, though as with any sort of topic I don't like when it feels it's being used as a crutch. This was present for me in the article. Complex jargon and concepts were used too much, to the point where I didn't understand how specific things functioned. This alone wouldn't have been a deal-breaker though, as I did manage to understand the big picture.

For purposes of this review, I'll note that I got ending B. The clues received didn't really lead me to that conclusion, and without being led through it by a character who made several leaps of logic that weren't explained in a way that made enough sense I would never have come to that conclusion with the small amount of evidence in my possession. Due to the fairly long length of the twine game and my disappointment at this ending, I did not feel that I wanted to go through the whole process again. Possibly due to its multiple endings, the clues picked up on the way just didn't feel like they were leading to a satisfying conclusion.

I'll also add that there were a lot of spelling errors, too many for me to point out individually. One that stuck out was the misspelling of pataphysicist in the second page of twine. The evidence log also rendered weirdly, with what looked like a stray > and one of my clues not actually appearing on it.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: wtf i love swann's proposal now!
item.content:encoded:
You managed to take my least favorite -001 proposal, and turn it into something that had me smiling from ear to ear, start to finish! Thank you!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
as a huge fan of pataphysics and metafictional work, this was Magnificient. i would +100 if i could. if this doesn't take the 5000 spot, i hope it's at least an iconic work. i know not many people like pataphysics, but as far as it goes i think this is one of the most well-done metafictional works on the site. i really dig the tie-in to swann's proposal.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn’t this basically Andrew swanns proposal? This clearly has more to give but still.

Edit: my bad you seem to be aware of this seeing as you reference it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually very much enjoyed the Twine section. So far I've got ending C and ending B (poor Bright…guess he got tired of getting used as a vent character so often.) Weirdly I got both of them twice after restarting, I guess I just pick things in those orders naturally? Interested in digging around for the other endings though.
